title: The Resilience of Yusuf
description: A 7-day journey through Surah Yusuf — patience, betrayal, and the triumph of trust in God.

quests.title: The Dream
quests.narrative: Yusuf shares a mysterious dream with his father — eleven stars, the sun, and the moon bow before him. Yaqub recognises its weight and warns his son to keep it secret.
quests.reflection: What do we do when we sense greatness stirring within us? Yaqub's wisdom: guard the gift before it is ready to meet the world.
quests.completionPrompt: Which emotion resonates with you most in these verses — wonder, fear, or hope?

quests.title: The Pit
quests.narrative: Jealousy ripens into action. His brothers throw Yusuf into the depths of a well, then return home with a bloodied shirt and a fabricated grief.
quests.reflection: Betrayal by those closest to us is among life's sharpest wounds. Yet even in the pit, God does not abandon His servant.
quests.completionPrompt: Have you ever found a moment of calm in what felt like rock bottom?

quests.title: The Caravan
quests.narrative: A caravan passing by draws water and finds a boy instead. Yusuf is sold into Egypt for a handful of coins — merchandise exchanged at the crossroads of destiny.
quests.reflection: God allowed Yusuf to be sold so He could place him exactly where he needed to be. Sometimes the worst transactions become divine positioning.
quests.completionPrompt: Reflect on a 'low price' moment in your life that later revealed greater purpose.

quests.title: The Palace Trial
quests.narrative: In Potiphar's household Yusuf rises — and is tested. The nobleman's wife pursues him; he flees to the door. Slander follows, and he is imprisoned despite his innocence.
quests.reflection: Yusuf's prayer in verse 33 is extraordinary: he does not ask for the woman to be stopped — he asks God to save him from his own potential weakness. True character is knowing where your vulnerabilities lie.
quests.completionPrompt: What is the difference between fleeing temptation and trusting God to guard you within it?

quests.title: The Prison
quests.narrative: Behind bars Yusuf becomes a counsellor. He interprets dreams for two companions and plants a seed of appeal in the one who will be freed — only to be forgotten for years.
quests.reflection: Doing good for others — even when you are yourself in chains — is among the greatest spiritual disciplines. Yusuf did not wait for freedom to be generous.
quests.completionPrompt: How do you maintain hope and purpose when you are waiting for someone else to act on your behalf?

quests.title: The King's Dream
quests.narrative: The king dreams of seven fat cows devoured by seven lean ones. Only Yusuf can interpret it. In one sentence he turns prison walls into a ministry of economics — and a gate opens.
quests.reflection: Years of waiting compressed into a single morning. When God moves, He moves completely. Yusuf did not waste the prison years; they became the credential that freed him.
quests.completionPrompt: What skills or wisdom are you developing in your current waiting season?

quests.title: Reunion
quests.narrative: Famine drives his brothers to Egypt. They stand before the governor not knowing it is Yusuf. He reveals himself — not with revenge but with grace. 'No blame on you today. God will forgive you.'
quests.reflection: The dream from Day 1 is fulfilled in Day 7. The stars bow, the circle closes. Yusuf's final words are not triumph but gratitude. The journey was never about Yusuf ascending — it was about God's faithfulness.
quests.completionPrompt: What would it mean to reach your highest point and respond with gratitude rather than vindication?
